translated: She conceived again and bore a son. And she said: Because the LORD heard that I was unloved, he has given me this one also. And she named him Simeon.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Eye Raxel gblɔ bena: Meʋli heƒo koko na Mawu le nɔvinye ta, meɖu dzi Eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Naftali.
translated: And Rachel said: I have wrestled and prayed to God for my sister, I prevailed Therefore she named him Naphtali.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Esi Yakob gbɔ tso gbe me le fiẽ la, Lea yi ɖakpee gblɔ bena: Gbɔnyee nava, elabena mexe fe ɖe tawo kple vinye ƒe agbitsa! Eye wòdɔ egbɔ le zã ma me.
translated: When Jacob came from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said: You must come to me, for I have hired you with my son's mandrakes! And he lay with her that night.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.
translated: Then Leah said that God has given her a good gift, now her husband will honor her, because she had borne him six sons. Therefore she named him Zebulun.

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Gbesigbe nava kpɔ nye fetua ɖa la, ekema nye anukwareɖiɖi adze ƒã; ne àkpɔ gbɔ̃, si mele ŋɔtaŋɔta o, kple esi metso kpɔ o, kpakple alẽvi, si mele yibɔ o la, ekema ɖe mefii.
translated: If my creditor comes to see me regularly, then my gratitude will be evident; if you see a goat that is not spotted, and one that has never been seen before, together with a lamb that is not at the breast, then I have fled.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Yakob bena: Trɔ yi fofowò ƒe anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ, eye manɔ anyi kpli wò!
translated: And the Lord said to Jacob: Return to the land of your father and to your relatives, and I will be with you!

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Eye wogblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, lãtsu, siwo me fli to, esiwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la, le lãnɔawo liam. Elabena mekpɔ nusi Laban wɔ ɖe ŋuwò la.
translated: And he said: Look, the male goats that mate with the flock are striped, spotted, and speckled. For I have seen all that Laban has done to you.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Ke esi Laban yi fu sẽ ge na lãwo la, Raxel fi fofoa ƒe aklama kpakpɛwo.
translated: But when Laban went to shear his sheep, Rachel stole her father's household idols.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Tete Laban ɖo ŋu na Yakob bena: Nyɔnuawo tɔnyewoe, ɖevia wo hã tɔnyewoe, lãwo tɔnyewoe, eye nusianu, si kpam nele la, tɔnyee! Ke nuka mawɔ ɖe vinyenyɔnuwo kple vi, siwo wodzi la ŋu?
translated: Then Laban answered Jacob: The women are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and everything you see is mine! But what can I do about my daughters or the children they have borne?

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Eye Laban na ŋkɔe be Yegar-Saxaduta; ke Yakob na ŋkɔe bena Galed.
translated: And Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Ne ate vinyenyɔnuwo ɖe anyi, alo naɖe srɔ̃ bubu akpe ɖe vinyenyɔnuawo ŋu la — amea ɖeke mele mia gbɔ o, Mawu koe nye ɖaseɖila le mia kpli wo dome.
translated: If he divorces the women, or marries another wife in addition to the women - there is no one with you, God alone is witness between you and them.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Elabena egblɔ bena: Ne Esau ava dze akpa ɖeka dzi, eye woade zi wo me la, ekema akpa ɖeka nasi adzo. Yakob ƒe gbedodoɖa.
translated: For he said: If Esau comes to the one part, and smites it, then the other part which is left shall escape. Jacob's prayer.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Eye Yakob na ŋkɔ teƒe la be Pniel, elabena egblɔ be: Mekpɔ Mawu ŋku me kple ŋku me: gake metsi agbe.
translated: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: for he said, I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Eye mekpɔ aƒenyiwo, tedziwo kple alẽwo, kluviwo kple kosiviwo, eye meɖo dɔlawo ɖo ɖe nye aƒetɔ gbɔ, be wòave nunye.
translated: I have acquired cattle, donkeys and sheep, male and female servants, and I have sent messengers to tell my lord, that I may find favor in your sight.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: nye medze wò nublanui, si nèkpɔ nam, kple wò anukware, si nẽɖi na wò dɔla la dometɔa ɖeke o; elabena nye ati sia metsɔ tso Yordan sia, ke azɔ la metrɔ zu asafokɔ eve.
translated: I am not worthy of all the kindness and faithfulness you have shown to your servant; for with only my staff I crossed this Jordan, but now I have become two camps.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Meɖe kuku, ɖem tso nɔvinye si me, tso Esau si me; elabena mele evɔ̃m be, ava dze dzinye, eye wòaɖe zi dadawo kple viwo dome.
translated: I pray thee, deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, and the mother with the children.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Ale miagblɔ na nye aƒetɔ Esau enye si: Wò dɔla Yakob be, míagblɔ na wò bena: Menɔ Laban gbɔ, eye metsi anyi ʋuu vaseɖe fifi.
translated: And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall ye speak unto my lord Esau; Thy servant Jacob saith thus, I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed there until now.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Elabena egblɔ bena: Ne Esau ava dze akpa ɖeka dzi, eye woade zi wo me la, ekema akpa ɖeka nasi adzo. Yakob ƒe gbedodoɖa.
translated: For he said: If Esau comes to one part and attacks them, then the other part will escape. Jacob's prayer.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Eye esi wòna woɖe tsi wo kple nusiwo katã le esi, yi ɖe tɔa godo la,
translated: And when he had sent them away with all that they had, he went to the sea,

ref: Ask anything, no matter how expensive. I'll do anything, just let me marry Dinah.”
text: Tabianu kple akpedanu, alesi nu miabiam la, mana, abe alesi miedi ene; mitsɔ ɖetugbivi la nam made ko.
translated: The dowry and the gift, whatever you ask of me, I will give, as much as you desire; only give me the young woman as a wife.

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Eye Yakob viŋutsuwo do ŋu na Sixem kple fofoa Xamor le aye me, elabena egblẽ wo nɔvinyɔnu Dina.
translated: Jacob's sons answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Negbe ne mianɔ abe miawo ene, eye mia dome ŋutsu sia ŋutsu natso aʋa la, eya ko hafi míalɔ̃.
translated: Unless you become like us, and every man among you is circumcised, only then will we agree.

ref: Finally, they reached Bethel, also known as Luz.
text: Eye Yakob kple amesiwo katã le eŋu la, wova Lus, esia nye Betel, le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.
translated: So Jacob and all the people with him came to Luz, that is Bethel, in the land of Canaan.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: Eye Yakob na ŋkɔ teƒe, si Mawu ƒo nu kplii le la, bena Betel. Raxel ƒe ku.
translated: Jacob named the place where God spoke with him Bethel. Rachel's death.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.
text: Eye Esau kplu srɔ̃awo, viaŋutsuwo kple vianyɔnuwo kple eƒe kluvi, siwo katã le eƒe me, kple eƒe lãhawo kple eƒe dɔwɔlãwo katã kpe ɖe eƒe kesinɔnu, siwo katã wòkpɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la ŋuti, eye woʋu le nɔvia Yakob gbɔ heyi ɖe Seir-nyigba dzi.
translated: Then Esau took his wives, his sons and daughters, and all the members of his household, as well as his livestock and all his other animals, and all the goods he had acquired in Canaan, and moved to a land some distance from his brother Jacob to Seir.

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lotan ƒe viŋutsuwo nye: Xori kple Hemam; eye Lotan nɔvinyɔnu enye Timna.
translated: Lotan's sons were: Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Esiawo nye Xoritɔwo ƒe amegãwo: Amegã Lotan, amegã Sobal, amegã Zibeon, amegã Ana,
translated: These are the chiefs of the Horites: Chief Lotan, chief Shobal, chief Zibeon, chief Anah,

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: amegã Magdiel kple amegã Iram. Esiawo nye Edom-megãwo le woƒe nɔƒewo kple woawo ŋutɔwo ƒe anyigbawonu. Eya nye Esau, amesi nye Edomtɔwo ƒe fofo.
translated: Chief Magdiel and Chief Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom according to their settlements in the land they possessed. This is Esau, who is the father of the Edomites.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Eye Israel lɔ̃ Yosef wu viaŋutsu bubuawo katã, elabena edzii le eƒe amegãkuku me, eye wòtɔ awu ʋlaya nɛ.
translated: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he was the son of his old age, and he made him a coat of many colors.

ref: pieces of silver they sold him to the Ishmaelites who took him to Egypt.
text: Mina miawui, eye miatsɔe aƒu gbe ɖe ɖoa ɖe me, eye míagblɔ bena, lã wodaa ɖe vuvui, ekema miakpɔ nusi eƒe drɔ̃e la ava zu.
translated: You shall grind it, and you shall throw it into the well, and we shall say that an animal fell into the well, then you will see what will become of his dreams.

ref: When the Midianite merchants came by, Joseph's brothers took him out of the well, and for
text: Ke Midian-sitsalawo va to wo ŋuti yi, eye woɖe Yosef le do la me hetsɔ dzra na Ismaeltɔwo klosalo blave, eye wokplɔ Yosef yi Egipte.
translated: But Midianite traders passed by, and they pulled Joseph up out of the pit and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver, and they took Joseph to Egypt.

ref: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
text: Esi Yuda ɖe ŋku ɖee nyuie la, egblɔ bena: Etɔ dzɔ wu tanye, elabena nyematsɔe na vinye Sela wòɖe o. Tso ema dzi la megade asi eŋu kpɔ o.
translated: When Judah recognized them, he said: She is more righteous than I, because I did not give her to my son Shelah to marry. From that time on he did not sleep with her again.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Eye wotsɔ eƒe nuwo katã de Yosef si, eye megatsɔ ɖeke le nyaa ɖeke me o, negbe eƒe nuɖuɖu ko. Eye Yosef nyo, eye wòdze ɖeka ŋutɔ. Yosef nɔ anyi dzadzɛ.
translated: He entrusted everything he had to Joseph, and he did not concern himself with anything except the food he ate. Now Joseph was well-built and handsome. Joseph sat down peacefully.

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: Eye wògblɔ nya mawo ke na eya hã bena: Kpɔ ɖa, Hebri-kluvi, si nekplɔ vɛ na mi la, va gbɔnye, be yeado ŋukpem.
translated: And she told him the same words, saying: The Hebrew servant, whom you brought to us, came to me to mock me.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Yosef ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Drɔ̃e la gɔme enye si: Alɔ etɔ̃awo nye ŋkeke etɔ̃.
translated: Joseph answered him saying: The dream's meaning is this: The three branches are three days.

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: Ke kusi etametɔ me la abolo vivi vovovowo li na Farao, eye xeviwo va le wo ɖum de kusia me le tanye.
translated: But in the top basket there were all kinds of baked goods for Pharaoh, and the birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Farao ɖu eƒe dzigbe, eye wòɖo kplɔ̃ na eƒe dɔlawo katã, eye wòɖe ahakulawo ƒe amegã kple aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã le gaxɔa me le eƒe dɔlawo katã ƒe ŋku me.
translated: On the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, he made a feast for all his servants, and he lifted up the head of the chief cupbearer and the head of the chief baker among his servants.

ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Le ƒe eve megbe la Farao ku drɔ̃e. Ele tsitre ɖe Nil to.
translated: Two years later, Pharaoh dreamed a dream. He was standing by the Nile River.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Eye Mawu kpɔ kekelia bena enyo. Eye Mawu ma kekeli la kple viviti la dome.
translated: And God saw the light, that it was good. And God divided the light from the darkness.

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Eye Mawu yɔ alilia be dziƒo. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke evelia.
translated: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke etɔ̃lia.
translated: And there was evening, and there was morning, the third day.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Eye Mawu wɔ ame ɖe ye ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme, Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wòwɔe ɖo; ŋutsu kple nyɔnu wòwɔ.
translated: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them.

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: ke gbewo kple amagbewo katã nanye nuɖuɖu na anyigba dzi lãwo katã kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo katã kple nusiwo katã tana, siwo me agbe le, le anyigba dzi. Eye wòva me nenema.
translated: And all the plants and all the trees shall be food for all the beasts of the earth and all the birds of the air and everything that creeps on the earth, in which there is life. And it was so.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Eye Mawu wu eƒe ɖo, si wòwɔ la nu le ŋkeke adreagbe, eye wòdzudzɔ le ŋkeke adreagbe tso eƒe do, siwo katã wòwɔ la me.
translated: And God finished His work which He had made on the seventh day, and He rested on the seventh day from all His work which He had done.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Esia nye dziƒo kple anyigba ƒe dzɔdzɔmenyawo le wo wɔwɔ me le ɣeyiɣi, si me Yehowa Mawu wo anyigba kple dziƒo.
translated: This is the history of the heavens and the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made the earth and the heavens.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu de se na ame gblɔ bena: Ɖu abɔmetiawo katã ƒe tsetse faa;
translated: And the Lord God commanded the man, saying: Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat;

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ bena: Menyo bena ame la natsi akogo o; mawɔ kpeɖeŋutɔ, si asɔ ɖe eŋu la nɛ.
translated: And the Lord God said: It is not good for the man to be alone; I will make a helper suitable for him.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Tete Yehowa Mawu tsɔ agbaƒuti, si wòɖe le amea me la, wɔ nyɔnui, eye wòkplɔe va ame la gbɔe.
translated: Then the LORD God made a woman from the rib he had taken out of the man, and he brought her to the man.

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Esi nyɔnu la kpɔ bena, ati la nyo na ɖuɖu, ebiã ŋu na ame, eye wòdzroa ame, eye wònana wodzea nunya la, egbe eƒe tsetsea ɖe heɖu, eye wòna srɔ̃a, si le egbɔ hã woɖu.
translated: When the woman saw that the tree was good for food, pleasing to the eye, and desirable for gaining wisdom, she took some of its fruit and ate it. She also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it.

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ na da la bena: Esi nèwɔ alea la, eyata woaƒo fi ade wò le aƒemelãwo kple gbemelãwo katã dome: Wò ƒodo dzi nazɔ, eye ʋuʋudedie naɖu le wò agbe me ŋkekewo katã me!
translated: Thus the Lord God said to the serpent: Because you have done this, you are cursed above all livestock and all wild animals: You shall crawl on your belly, and you shall eat dust all the days of your life!

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Eye wògblɔ na nyɔnua bena: Mana nakpe fu geɖe le fufɔfɔ me, alé ku hafi àdzi vi, ke wò tame anɔ srɔ̃wò ŋu, eye eya anye gã ɖe dziwò.
translated: And he said to the woman: I will greatly multiply your pain in childbearing, you will suffer before giving birth to a child, but your desire will be for your husband, and he will rule over you.

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Eye ame la tsɔ ŋkɔ na srɔ̃a bena Xawa, elabena eya ava nye ame gbagbewo katã dada.
translated: The man named his wife Eve, because she would become the mother of all the living.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ be: Kpɔ ɖa, ame zu sigbe mía dometɔ ɖeka ene, eye wònya nyui kple vɔ. Ke be wòagaɖo asi ɖa agbe agbetia hã ƒe ɖe aɖu, eye wòanɔ agbe ɖoɖoɖo o la,
translated: And the LORD God said: Behold, the man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil. And now, lest he put forth his hand and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live forever—

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: ke Kain kple eƒe nunana ya medze eŋu o. Eye Kain bi dzi ŋutɔ, eye wòɖo adã.
translated: but Cain and his offering he did not accept. And Cain was very angry, and his countenance fell.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Kain bena: Nukaŋuti nèle dzi bim, eye nukaŋuti nèle adã ɖom?
translated: Then the Lord said to Cain: Why are you angry, and why has your countenance fallen?

ref: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
text: Wòe nyãm egbe tso agbledenyigba la dzi; madzo le wò ŋkuta anye tsatsala kple godzela le anyigba dzi, eye ava me bena, amesiame, si akpɔm la, awum.
translated: You are driving me today from the face of the earth; I shall be hidden from your presence and I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and it will come to pass that anyone who finds me will kill me.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Eye wodzi Irad na Henox, eye Irad dzi Mehuyael, eye Mehuyael dzi Metusael, eye Metusael dzi Lamex.
translated: And Irad was born to Enoch, and Irad begot Mehujael, and Mehujael begot Methushael, and Methushael begot Lamech.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Eye Zila hã dzi Tubalkain, amesi nye akɔbli- kple gaŋudowolawo fofo; eye Tubalkain nɔvinyɔnu ŋkɔ nye Naama.
translated: And Zillah also bore Tubal-cain, who was the father of all those who work with bronze and iron; and Tubal-cain's sister's name was Naamah.

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: Eye Lamex gblɔ na srɔ̃wo bena: Ada kple Zila, miɖo to nye gbe; mi Lamex srɔ̃wo, mise ye nyawo! Vavã, mewu ŋutsu ɖe nye abi teƒe kple ɖekakpui ɖe nye dodro teƒe.
translated: Then Lamech said to his wives: Adah and Zillah, listen to my voice; wives of Lamech, hear my words! For I have killed a man for wounding me, and a young man for striking me.

ref: God created men and women to be like himself. He gave them his blessing and called them human beings. The following is a list of the descendants of Adam, the first man:
text: Ewɔ wo wonye ŋutsu kple nyɔnu, eye wòyra wo: eye wòna ŋkɔ wo bena „ame“ le gbesigbe wòwɔ wo.
translated: He created them male and female, and blessed them: and called their name "man" in the day when he created them.

ref: Then the Lord said, “I won't let my life-giving breath remain in anyone forever. No one will live for more than
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Nye gbɔgbɔ maganɔ amewo me boo o, elabena ŋutilã wonye le woƒe vodada me, eye woƒe agbe me ŋkekewo nanye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blave!
translated: And the LORD said: My Spirit shall not abide in man forever, for he is flesh in his waywardness, and his days shall be one hundred and twenty years!

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: Le ŋkeke mawo me la ame dzɔtsuwo le anyigba dzi; eye esi Mawu viŋutsuwo ɖe amegbetɔwo ƒe vinyɔnuwo, eye wodzi vi na wo megbe la hã wogale; woawo nye ame kako ŋkɔxɔla, siwo nɔ agbe le blema.
translated: In those days there were giants on the earth; and also after that, when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bore children to them, the same became mighty men which were of old, men of renown.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Eye Noa wɔe; alesi Mawu de se nɛ la, nenema pɛ wòwɔ.
translated: Noah did it; just as God commanded him, so he did.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ke mabia nu kpli wò bena, nayi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃wò hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋu.
translated: But I will establish my covenant with you, and you shall enter the ark, you and your sons and your wife and your sons' wives with you.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Ɖe lã matsrimatsriwo katã atsu kple asi adreadre, kple lã tsitsriwo atsu kple asi eveveve.
translated: All the small animals will be caught with fifteen hands, and the big animals will be caught with four hands.

ref: The Lord destroyed everything that breathed. Nothing was left alive except Noah and the others in the boat.
text: Nusianu, si nɔ anyigba dzi, tso amewo dzi vaseɖe aƒemelãwo kple nu tatawo kpakple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo dzi, wotsrɔ̃ wo ɖa le anyigba dzi; negbe Noa kple nusiwo le egbɔ le aɖakaʋua me koe tsi agbe.
translated: Everything on the earth, from people to livestock and creeping things and birds of the sky, was destroyed from the earth; only Noah and those with him in the ark survived.

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: Do go le aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ kple srɔ̃wo kple viwòŋutsuwo hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋuti!
translated: Enter the ark, you yourself and your wife and your sons together with your sons' wives!

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Tete Noa do go, eye viaŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃a kpakple viaŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo hã kpe ɖe eŋuti.
translated: Then Noah went out, and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Tso esia dzi, ŋkeke alesi anyigba li la, nuƒaƒã kple nuŋeŋe, viwo kple fifia, dzomeŋɔli kple pepi, ŋkeke kple zã nu matsi o.
translated: From now on, as long as the earth exists, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night shall not cease.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Eye ava me bena, ne mele ƒu ƒom alilikpowo le dziƒo, eye anyieʋɔ ɖi ɖe alilikpoawo ŋu lã,
translated: And it will come to pass that, when I bring clouds over the sky, and the rainbow appears in the clouds,

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Noa bena: Esia enye nubabla, si meɖo anyi le mia kple ŋutilã, siwo katã le anyigba dzi dome la ƒe dzesi.
translated: And God said to Noah: This is the sign of the covenant which I have established between me and all flesh that is on the earth.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Eye Noa de asi agbledede me, eye wòde weingblea ɖe.
translated: Noah began farming and planted a vineyard.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Eye Xam, Kanaan fofo, kpɔ fofoa ƒe ama, eye wòɖagblɔe na nɔvia eveawo le xexe.
translated: Ham, the father of Canaan, saw his father's nakedness and told his two brothers outside.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: egblɔ bena: Woaƒo fi ade Kanaan, kluviwo ƒe kluvi wòanye na nɔviawo!
translated: He said: Cursed be Canaan, he shall be a servant of servants to his brothers!

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Eye wògagblɔbena: Woakafu Yehowa, Sem ƒe Mawu; eye Kanaan anye kluvi na wo!
translated: And he said: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem; and let Canaan be his servant!

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Tete Yehowa ɖi va anyi, be yeakpɔ du kple mɔ̃ kɔkɔ, si amegbetɔviwo le tutum la ɖa. Mawu tɔtɔ amewo ƒe gbe.
translated: Then Jehovah came down to see the city and the tall tower that the sons of men were building. God confused the people's language.

ref: Please save my life by saying you are my sister.”
text: Gblɔ ko dzro bena, nɔvinyenyɔnu nènye, ne manɔ dedie le tawò, eye matsi agbe le tawò!
translated: Just say, be my sister, so that it may go well with me because of you, and my life may be spared on your account!

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Eye Farao do amewo, bena woakplɔ eya kple srɔ̃a kple etɔwo katã aɖo ɖa.
translated: And Pharaoh commanded his men concerning him, and they sent him away with his wife and all that he had.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Ke Sodomtɔwo nye ame baɖawo, eye woda vo geɖe ɖe Yehowa ŋuti. Mawu gado ŋugbe na Abram.
translated: But the people of Sodom were wicked, and they sinned greatly against Jehovah. God made a promise to Abram.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Elabena anyigba blibo la katã, si kpam nèle la, matsɔe ana wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo tegbetegbe.
translated: For all the land which you see, I will give it to you and to your descendants forever.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene ale be, ne amea ɖe ate ŋu axlẽ ʋuʋudedi la, eya koe ate ŋu axlẽ wo dzidzimeviwo hã.
translated: I will make your descendants as numerous as the dust of the earth, so that if anyone could count the dust, then your descendants could also be counted.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Eye wòva me le ŋkeke mawo me bena, Amrafel, Sinear-fia, Ariok, Elasar-fia, Kedorlaomer, Elam-fia kple Tideal, Goyim-fia,
translated: And it came to pass in those days that Amraphel, king of Shinar, Arioch, king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer, king of Elam, and Tidal, king of Goiim,

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: kple Xoritɔwo le woƒe to Seir dzi vaseɖe El-Paran, si te ɖe gbedzi lɔƒo.
translated: and the Horites in their Mount Seir as far as El-paran, which is by the wilderness.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Emegbe wòtrɔ va En-Mispat, si enye Kades, eye wòsi Amalekitɔwo katã kple Amoritɔ, siwo nɔ Xazazon-Tamar la hã.
translated: Then he returned to En-Mishpat, which is Kadesh, and conquered all the territory of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Ke aŋɔʋe geɖewo le Sidim-gbadzaƒe la. Esi Sodom-kple Gomora-fia wòsi la, woge ɖe wo me; ke ame mamleawo si yi to dzi.
translated: But there were many tar pits in the Valley of Siddim. When the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, they fell into them; but the rest fled to the mountains.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Tete amea ɖe si va gblɔe na Hebritɔ Abram, si le Amoritɔ Mamre, Eskol kple Aner nɔvi ƒe ali gãwo te la. Ame siawoe bia nu kple Abram.
translated: Then one who had escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew, who was dwelling by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and Aner. These were allies of Abram.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Ke Melxizedek, Salem-fia, tsɔ abolo kple wein vɛ; ame sia enye Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ ƒe nunɔla.
translated: But Melchizedek, king of Salem, brought bread and wine; this man was a priest of God Most High.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Eye wòyrae gblɔ bena Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ, si wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba, nayra Abram!
translated: And he blessed him and said, Blessed be Abram by God Most High, who made heaven and earth!

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: bena, nyemaxɔ naneke atso nusiwo katã nye tɔwo la me o, ka loo, alo atokotaka gɔ̃ hã o, bena nagagblɔ bena, yee wɔ Abram wòzu kesinɔtɔ o!
translated: so that I will not take anything from all that is yours, not even a thread or a sandal strap, so that you cannot say, 'I made Abram rich!'

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Tete xe ƒonuwo va dze ɖe lãwo dzi, ke Abram nyã wo ɖa.
translated: Then birds of prey came down on the carcasses, but Abram drove them away.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Hafi wò dzidzime enelia agatrɔ ava afisia, elabena Amoritɔwo ƒe vodada mesɔ gbɔ vɔ haɖe o.
translated: Before your fourth generation returns here, because the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Eye Abram srɔ̃ Sarai medzi vi nɛ kpɔ o. Ke Egipte-nyɔnua ɖe, si woyɔna be Hagar la, nye eƒe kosi.
translated: Now Abram's wife Sarai had borne him no children. But she had an Egyptian maidservant named Hagar.

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Eyata woyɔ vudo ma bena: Agbetɔ, si le kpɔyem la ƒe vudo; ele Kades kple Bared dome.
translated: Therefore that well was called: The well of the One who sees me; it is between Kadesh and Bered.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Eye nye nubabla nanɔ mia kpli wò dome, eye mana nasɔ gbɔ ɖe edzi.
translated: There will be compassion between you and him, and he will have mercy again.

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Tete Abram tsyɔ mo anyi, eye Mawu ƒo nu kplii bena:
translated: Then Abram fell on his face, and God spoke with him saying:

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: Eye mana nadzi ɖe dzi ŋutɔ, eye dukɔwo kple fiawo adzɔ tso mewo.
translated: She shall indeed bear a son, and nations and kings shall come from her.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: Eye maɖo nye nubabla anyi le mía kpli wò kple megbewòviwo katã dome, eye wòanye nubabla mavɔ, bena manye wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo ƒe Mawu tegbee.
translated: And I will establish my covenant between me and you and your descendants after you throughout their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your descendants after you.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Abraham bena: Ke wò la lé ye nubabla la me ɖe asi; wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo katã milé ye nubabla la me ɖe asi!
translated: And God said to Abraham: But you shall keep my covenant; you yourself and your descendants after you shall keep my covenant!

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ismael netsi agbe na wò ko! Mawu ɖo Isak ŋugbe na Abraham.
translated: Then Abraham said to God: If only Ishmael might live under your blessing! God promised Isaac to Abraham.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Ke mabia nu kple Isak, amesi Sara adzi na wò le ƒe, si gbɔna ƒe woalèwoɣi la.
translated: But I will establish my covenant with Isaac, whom Sarah will bear to you at this set time next year.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Eye Abraham kple Sara wotsi xɔ ƒe geɖe ŋutɔ, ale bena Sara megaléa gbe gɔ̃ hã o.
translated: Now Abraham and Sara were very old, so that Sara no longer even menstruated.

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: Tete Sara sẽ nu gblɔ bena: Nyemeko nu o; elabena ele vɔvɔ̃m. Ke egblɔ ne bena: O, èko nu tso! Sodom gbegblẽ gbɔgblɔɖi.
translated: Then Sarah denied it, saying: I did not laugh; because she was afraid. But he said to her: No, you did laugh! Sodom's destruction proclamation.

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Eye wodo ɣli ɖe eta gblɔ bena: Te ɖa afia! Wò amedzro ɖeka hã sia, si va dze mía gbã la, nanɔ ɖokuiwò wum amegãe alea? Azɔ la miawo vo ɖe wòa ŋuwò wu woawo gɔ̃. Eye wonyẽ zi ɖe Lot dzi gbidigbidi, be yewoagbã ʋɔa.
translated: Then they shouted at him and said: Stand back! This stranger of yours who came to us first, does he want to make himself a great judge like this? Now we will do worse to you than to them. And they pressed hard against Lot, to break down the door.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Ke ŋutsuawo do abɔ ɖa he Lot va wo ɖokui gbee le aƒea me, eye wotu ʋɔa.
translated: But the men reached out and pulled Lot back into the house to them, and shut the door.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Ɖe abla yi afima kaba; elabena nyemate ŋu awɔ naneke o, vaseɖe esime naɖo afima. Eyata woyɔa ɖu la be Zoar.
translated: Flee there quickly; for I cannot do anything until you arrive there. Therefore the name of the city was called Zoar.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Esi ŋu ke la, tsitsitɔ la gblɔ na ɖevitɔ bena: Eto ƒe zã la memlɔ fofonye gbɔ. Míagana wòano wein egbe ƒe zã hã; ekema wò hã nayi aɖamlɔ egbɔ, bena mía fofo nado fu mí míadzi vi.
translated: Then the older daughter said to the younger: "Last night I slept with my father. Let us make him drink wine tonight also; then you also go and sleep with him, so that our father may give us children."

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Eye ɖevitɔ hã dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ ne be Ben-Ami, eya nye Amonitɔwo tɔgbui.
translated: The younger daughter also bore a son, and she named him Ben-Ammi; he is the father of the Ammonites to this day.

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Tete Mawu gblɔ nɛ le drɔ̃eƒe bena: Nye hã menyae bena, wò dzime kɔ le ewɔwɔ me; eyata metso wò xɔ ɖo, be nagade asi eŋu ahawɔ nuvɔ̃ ɖe ŋunye o.
translated: Then God said to him in the dream: I also know that you did this with a pure heart; therefore I prevented you from touching her to sin against me.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Ke Yehowa ɖo ŋku ŋugbe, si wòɖo na Sara la dzi, eye Yehowa wɔ na Sara, abe alesi wògblɔ ene.
translated: But Jehovah turned his attention to the promise he made to Sarah, and Jehovah did for Sarah just as he had said.

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Ameka susu le Abraham ŋuti bena, Sara ana no viwò mahã? Elabena medzi ŋutsuvi nɛ le eƒe amegãkuku me.
translated: And he said: Who would have thought concerning Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? For she bore him a son in his old age.

ref: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Abraham bena: Megana nya sia nave wò le ŋutsuvi la kple wò kosi la ta o; ke boŋ ɖo to Sara le nusianu, si wodi tso gbɔwò la me, elabena Isak me wò dzidzime-viwo adzo tso.
translated: And God said to Abraham: Let not this thing be grievous to you because of the boy and because of your bondwoman; but in all that Sarah has said to you, listen to her voice, for in Isaac shall your seed be called.

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Esi lãgbalẽgolo me tsi la vɔ la, etsɔ ɖevi la yi ɖamlɔ ati dodoea ɖe te,
translated: When the eagle had finished eating, it took the child and laid it down under a tall tree,

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ bena: Melɔ̃, maka atam la.
translated: And Abraham said: I agree, I will swear the oath.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Eye esi wòbia nu le Beer-Seba la, Abimelex kple eƒe aʋafia Pixol wodze mɔ gatrɔ yi ɖe Filistitowo ƒe anyigba dzi.
translated: After he had made a request at Beersheba, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol returned to the land of the Philistines.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Eye Abraham do atiwo ɖe Beer-Seba, eye wòyɔ Yehowa Mawu, si le agbe tegbee la ƒe ŋkɔ dzi le afima.
translated: And Abraham planted trees in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of the LORD, the everlasting God.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Ke Abraham nye amedzro le Filistitowo ƒe anyigba dzi eteƒe didi.
translated: But Abraham lived as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines for a long time.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Le nya siawo megbe la Mawu do Abraham kpɔ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Abraham! Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nye enye si!
translated: After these things God tested Abraham, and said to him: Abraham! And he answered: Here I am!

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Tete Abraham gblɔ na dɔlawo bena: Mitsi afisia kple tedzi la; ke mía kple vinye la míayi afime aɖado gbe ɖa, emegbe míagatrɔ ava mia gbɔ.
translated: Then Abraham said to the servants: Stay here with the donkey; and I and the boy will go over there and worship, and then we will come back to you.

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: eyata mayra wò geɖe ŋutɔ, eye viwòwo asɔ gbɔ fũ abe dziƒoɣletiviwo kple ƒutake ene, eye wò dzidzimeviwo atsɔ woƒe futɔwo ƒe duwo anyi domee.
translated: therefore I will surely bless you greatly, and your children will multiply abundantly like the stars of heaven and the sand of the seashore, and your descendants will possess the gates of their enemies.

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Eye srɔ̃ evelia ŋkɔ nye Rehuma, eya hã dzi Tebax, Gaham, Tahas kple Maxa nɛ.
translated: His second wife's name was Reumah, and she also bore him Tebah, Gaham, Tahash and Maacah.

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: Amedzro menye le mia dome, mina ameɖiƒem, bena matsɔ nye ame kuku la aɖi ɖe afima!
translated: I am a stranger and sojourner among you; give me property for a burying place among you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight!

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Tete dɔla la ɖe kposɔ ewo tso eƒe aƒetɔ ƒe kposɔwo dome, eye wotsɔ eƒe aƒetɔ ƒe nu vevi vovovowo ɖe asi, eye wòdze mɔ heyi Mesopotamia, ɖe Naxor ƒe ɖu la me.
translated: Then the servant took ten camels from his master's camels, and departed with various valuable things of his master in his hand, and he arose and went to Mesopotamia, to the city of Nahor.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Kpɔ ɖa, mele tsitre ɖe vudoa to, eye ne dua me nyɔnuviwo va tsi du ge,
translated: Behold, I am standing by the spring, and when the young women of the city come out to draw water,

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Ke ŋutsu la zi kpi le ekpɔm dũ, ne yeadze sii be, ɖe Yehowa na yeƒe mɔzɔzɔ dze dzi loo, alo medze edzi o.
translated: But the man kept staring at her in silence, to find out whether the LORD had made his journey successful or not.

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Ke nyɔnuvi la ƒu du yi aƒe me, be woaɖagblɔ nya siawo na dadaa.
translated: But the girl ran home to tell these things to her mother.

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: eye woaɖo ŋu nam be: Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã la, eya nanye nyɔnu, si Yehowa ɖo ɖi na nye aƒetɔ ƒe vi la!
translated: and she will answer me: Drink, and I will also give water to your camels, let her be the woman whom Jehovah has appointed for my master's son!

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Azɔ, ne miedi be, miave nye aƒetɔ nu, eye miaɖi anukware nɛ la, ekema migblɔe nam; ke ne mielɔ̃ o la, migblɔe nam, bena nenye ɖusi me alo mia mee hã, ne maɖo tae.
translated: Now, if you are willing to show kindness and faithfulness to my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, so I may know which way to turn.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Esia megbe la eya kple ŋutsu, siwo kpe ɖe eŋu la, woɖu nu, eye wono nu, eye wotsi dɔ. Ke esi wofɔ ŋdi la, egblɔ bena: Mido mɔm, ne mayi nye aƒetɔ gbɔ!
translated: After this, he and the men who were with him ate and drank, and spent the night. But when they rose in the morning, he said: Send me away, that I may go to my master!

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Eye nɔvia kple dadaa gblɔ bena: Na nyɔnuvi la nanɔ mía gbɔ ʋee abe ŋkeke ewo ene, emegbe la ayi faa.
translated: Her brother and her mother said: Let the young woman stay with us a while, about ten days; after that she may go.

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Tete woyɔ Rebeka vɛ, eye wobiae bena: ayi kple ŋutsu sia? Eɖo eŋu bena: E, mayi.
translated: Then they called Rebekah and asked her: Will you go with this man? She answered: Yes, I will go.

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Esia megbe la dɔla la xlẽ nusiwo katã wòwɔ la fia Isak.
translated: After this, the servant told Isaac all the things he had done.

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Eye Yakob gblɔ be: Ka atam ƒu dzi nam gbɔ! Tete wòka atam nɛ, eye wòtsɔ eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye ƒle na Yakob.
translated: And Jacob said: Swear to me this day! Then he swore to him, and he sold his birthright to Jacob.

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: Eye Isak nɔ Gerar.
translated: And Isaac lived in Gerar.

ref: Isaac planted grain and had a good harvest that same year. The Lord blessed him,
text: Eye Isak ƒã nu ɖe anyigba ma dzi eye wòŋe nu le ƒe ma me wòdzi ɖe dzi zi alafa ɖeka sɔŋ, elabena Yehowa yrae.
translated: And Isaac sowed in that land and reaped in that same year a hundredfold, because the Lord blessed him.

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Eye Abimelex gblɔ na Isak bena: Dzo le mía gbɔ, elabena ekpɔ ŋusẽ ɖe mía dzi akpa!
translated: Then Abimelech said to Isaac: Go away from us, because you have become too powerful for us!

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Eyata tsɔ wò adedanuwo, ɖaku kple da, eye nayi gbe me aɖawu lãa ɖe vɛ nam!
translated: Therefore take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me!

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: Eyata vinye, se nye gbe, eye nawɔ nusi magblɔ na wò la!
translated: Therefore my child, listen to my voice, and do what I tell you!

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Tete Yakob gblɔ na dadaa Rebeka bena: Enyo hafi, gake nɔvinye Esau la ɖu me wònye, ke nye la fu mele ŋunye o.
translated: Then Jacob said to his mother Rebekah: That's fine, but my brother Esau is hairy, while I have no hair.

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: Dadaa ɖo ŋu ne bena: Vinye, nye boŋ ŋue wò fiƒoɖe la laɖi; ɖo tom ko, eye nayi aɖalée vɛ!
translated: The father answered him saying: My son, I myself will provide the lamb for the burnt offering; just follow me, and go and catch it and bring it!

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Tete Isak gblɔ na via bena: Aleke wɔ nèkpɔe kaba ale, vinye? Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Elabena wò Mawu la na wòva ɖo gom.
translated: Then Isaac said to his son: How did you find it so quickly, my son? He answered him saying: Because your God caused it to come to me.

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Esi wote ɖe eŋu ɖuɖɔ nu nɛ la, eʋẽ eƒe awu me se; eye wòyrae gblɔ bena: Ehɛ̃, vinye le ʋeʋẽm abe alesi gbe, si Yehowa yra, ʋẽnae ene.
translated: When he came near to him, he felt his clothing; and he blessed him and said: Behold, my son smells like a field which Jehovah has blessed.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Mawu ana dziƒozãmu kple anyigba wɔnu wò, eye àkpɔ bli kple wein geɖe fũ ŋutɔ.
translated: God will give you abundance from heaven and earth, and you will have plenty of grain and wine.

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Wò amekae? Eɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye Esau, wò ŋgɔgbevi.
translated: His father Isaac said to him: Who are you? He answered: I am Esau, your firstborn.

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Eɖo ŋu bena: Yakob ye woyɔnɛ vavã! Zi eve enye si wòblem: Exɔ ŋgɔgbevinyenye le asinye, azɔ egaxɔ nye yayra hã! Eye wòbiae bena: Mèkpɔtɔ yayraa ɖeke ɖi nam oa?
translated: And he said: Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times: he took away my birthright; and, behold, now he has taken away my blessing. And he said, Have you not reserved a blessing for me?

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Esi Rebeka se via xoxoetɔ Esau ƒe nyawo la, ena woyɔ via ɖevitɔ Yakob vɛ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, nɔviwò Esau ɖo be, yeabia hlɔ̃ awu wò.
translated: When Rebekah heard the words of her older son Esau, she sent for her younger son Jacob and said to him: Look, your brother Esau is planning to take revenge by killing you.

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: eye bena Yakob ɖo to fofoa kple dadaa, eye wòyi Mesopotamia la,
translated: and that Jacob obeyed his father and mother and went to Mesopotamia,

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Eye wòkpɔ Yehowa le tsitre ɖe eŋgɔ gblɔ bena: Nye enye Yehowa, fofowò Abraham ƒe Mawu kple Isak ƒe Mawu: Anyigba, si dzi nèmlɔ la, màtsɔe ana mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo.
translated: And he saw the LORD standing before him saying: I am the LORD, the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac: the land on which you lie, I will give it to you and your descendants.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: Eye wò dzidzimeviwo asɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene, eye nakeke ta ɖe ɣetoɖoƒe, ɣedzeƒe, anyiehe kple dziehe, eye woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã. Ne mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo me.
translated: And your descendants shall be as the dust of the earth, and you shall spread abroad to the west, and to the east, and to the north, and to the south: and in you and in your seed shall all the families of the earth be blessed.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Eye Yakob ɖe adzɔgbe gblɔ bena: Ne Mawu anɔ anyi kplim, eye wòakpɔ dzinye le mɔ, si dzi mele la dzi, eye wòana abolom maɖu kple awum mado,
translated: And Jacob made a vow saying: If God will be with me, and will watch over me on this journey that I am taking, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to wear,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: eye wòana maganɔ ava fofonye ƒe me dedie la, ekema Yehowa anye nye Mawu.
translated: and this stone which I have set up as a pillar will be God's house, then the LORD will be my God.

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: Egblɔ na wo bena: Mienyã Naxor vi Laban mahã? Woɖo ŋu bena: Ẽ, mienyae!
translated: He said to them: Do you know Nahor's son Laban? They answered: Yes, we know him!

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Eye Yakob gblɔ na Raxel bena, fofoa nɔvi yenye, eye Rebeka vi yenye; tete wòƒu ɖu yi ɖagblɔe na fofoa.
translated: Then Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's relative, and that he was Rebekah's son; and she ran and told her father.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Tete Laban gblɔ nɛ bena: Nyateƒee, nɔvinye kpamkpam nènye! Ke esi wònɔ egbɔ ɣleti ɖeka la,
translated: Then Laban said to him: "Truly, you are my own brother!" But when he had been with him for a month,

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Ale Yakob subɔ ƒe adre ɖe Raxel ta, eye wòle nɛ abe ŋkekea ɖewo ko ene, le alesi gbegbe wòlɔ̃ne la ta. Yakob ɖe srɔ̃.
translated: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed to him but a few days because of his love for her. Jacob married.

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Eyome la egafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi. Eye wògblɔ bena: Yehowa se bena, womelɔ̃a nye nya o; eyata wotso esia hã nam. Eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Simeon.
translated: She conceived again and bore a son. And she said: Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, he has given me this one also. And she named him Simeon.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Eyome la Raxel ƒe kosi Bilha gafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi evelia na Yakob.
translated: Rachel's servant Bilhah conceived again, and bore a second son to Jacob.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Eye Raxel gblɔ bena: Meʋli heƒo koko na Mawu le nɔvinye ta, meɖu dzi Eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Naftali.
translated: And Rachel said: I have wrestled and prayed to God for my sister, I prevailed. Therefore she named him Naphtali.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Esi Yakob gbɔ tso gbe me le fiẽ la, Lea yi ɖakpee gblɔ bena: Gbɔnyee nava, elabena mexe fe ɖe tawo kple vinye ƒe agbitsa! Eye wòdɔ egbɔ le zã ma me.
translated: When Jacob came from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said: You must come to me, for I have hired you with my son's mandrakes! And he lay with her that night.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.
translated: Then Leah said that God had given her a good gift, now her husband would honor her, because she had borne him six sons. Therefore she named him Zebulun.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Emegbe la edzi nyɔnuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe be Dina.
translated: Later she gave birth to a daughter, and named her Dinah.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Eye wòfɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, tete wògblɔ bena: Mawu ɖe nye ŋukpe ɖa!
translated: Then she gave birth to a son, and she said: God has taken away my disgrace!

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Ke esi Raxel dzi Yosef megbe la, Yakob gblɔ na Laban bena: Do mɔm, ne mayi mía de.
translated: But after Rachel gave birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban: Send me away, so that I may go to our place.

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Tete wògblɔ bena: Nuka mana wò? Yakob ɖo ŋu bena: Nyemaxɔ agaga femee hã le asiwò o, negbe ne alɔ nusi magblɔ na wò la dzi ko; na maganyi wò alẽwo, eye makpɔ wo dzi.
translated: Then he said: What shall I give you? Jacob answered: You shall not give me anything at all, except if you agree to what I will tell you; let me return to tend your sheep, and I will take care of them.

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Gbesigbe nava kpɔ nye fetua ɖa la, ekema nye anukwareɖiɖi adze ƒã; ne àkpɔ gbɔ̃, si mele ŋɔtaŋɔta o, kple esi metso kpɔ o, kpakple alẽvi, si mele yibɔ o la, ekema ɖe mefii.
translated: If a stranger comes to see my wife, then my suspicion will be confirmed; if you see a goat that is not pregnant, and one that has not given birth, together with a lamb that is not suckling, then I am a thief.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Eye Yakob ɖe lãviawo ɖo Laban ƒe lã, siwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la kasa. Ale wòɖo lãha tɔxe na eɖokui, eye mena wotɔtɔ kple Laban towo o.
translated: Jacob set apart the young of the flock to Laban's animals that were striped and spotted. So he made separate flocks for himself, and did not put them with Laban's animals.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Yakob bena: Trɔ yi fofowò ƒe anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ, eye manɔ anyi kpli wò!
translated: And the Lord said to Jacob: Return to the land of your father and to your relatives, and I will be with you!

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Eye wogblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, lãtsu, siwo me fli to, esiwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la, le lãnɔawo liam. Elabena mekpɔ nusi Laban wɔ ɖe ŋuwò la.
translated: And he said: Lift up now thine eyes, and see, all the rams which leap upon the cattle are ringstraked, speckled, and grisled: for I have seen all that Laban doeth unto thee.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Ke esi Laban yi fu sẽ ge na lãwo la, Raxel fi fofoa ƒe aklama kpakpɛwo.
translated: But when Laban went to shear his sheep, Rachel stole her father's household idols.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Tete wotsɔ nɔvia wo kpe ɖe eɖokui ŋu ti eyome ŋkeke adre sɔŋ, eye wòva tui le Gilead-towo dzi.
translated: Then his brothers and all his father's household went down and took him up and buried him between Zorah and Eshtaol in the tomb of Manoah his father.

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Eye Laban va tu Yakob; eye Yakob tu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe to la dzi, ke Laban kple nɔviawo tu wo tɔ ɖe Gilead-to la dzi.
translated: And Laban overtook Jacob; and Jacob had pitched his tent in the mount, and Laban with his brethren pitched in the mount of Gilead.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Tete Laban gblɔ na Yakob bena: Nuka nye esi nèwɔ, eye nèblem hekplɔ vinyenyɔnuwo dzoe abe aboyomewo ene?
translated: Then Laban said to Jacob: What have you done, that you deceived me and carried away my daughters like captives?

ref: You didn't even give me a chance to kiss my own grandchildren and daughters goodbye. That was really foolish.
text: eye mèna meɖuɖɔ nu na nye tɔgbuiyɔviwo kple vinyenyɔnuwo o mahã? Ewɔ bometsinu!
translated: so I shouldn't cook for my grandchildren and great-grandchildren? That's nonsense!

ref: Jacob answered, “I left secretly because I was afraid you would take your daughters from me by force.
text: Tete Yakob ɖo ŋu na Laban bena: Mele vɔvɔ̃m, elabena mesusu be, ɖewohĩ axa viwònyɔnuawo le asinye.
translated: Then Jacob answered Laban: I was afraid, because I thought that perhaps you would take your daughters from me by force.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Tete Laban ɖo ŋu na Yakob bena: Nyɔnuawo tɔnyewoe, ɖevia wo hã tɔnyewoe, lãwo tɔnyewoe, eye nusianu, si kpam nele la, tɔnyee! Ke nuka mawɔ ɖe vinyenyɔnuwo kple vi, siwo wodzi la ŋu?
translated: Then Laban answered Jacob: The women are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and everything you see is mine! But what can I do about my daughters or the children they have borne?

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Eye Laban na ŋkɔe be Yegar-Saxaduta; ke Yakob na ŋkɔe bena Galed.
translated: And Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Ne ate vinyenyɔnuwo ɖe anyi, alo naɖe srɔ̃ bubu akpe ɖe vinyenyɔnuawo ŋu la — amea ɖeke mele mia gbɔ o, Mawu koe nye ɖaseɖila le mia kpli wo dome.
translated: If he divorces the women, or marries another wife in addition to the women — there is no one among you, God alone is the witness between you and them.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Elabena egblɔ bena: Ne Esau ava dze akpa ɖeka dzi, eye woade zi wo me la, ekema akpa ɖeka nasi adzo. Yakob ƒe gbedodoɖa.
translated: For he said: If Esau comes to one part and attacks them, then the other part will escape. Jacob's prayer.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Meɖe kuku, ɖem tso nɔvinye si me, tso Esau si me; elabena mele evɔ̃m be, ava dze dzinye, eye wòaɖe zi dadawo kple viwo dome.
translated: I pray thee, deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, and the mother with the children.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Nenema wògblɔ na ame evelia, etɔ̃lia kple mamle, siwo katã le lãhawo kplɔm la, eye wògblɔ bena: Alea tututu miagblɔ na Esau, ne mieɖo goe!
translated: He gave the same instructions to the second, the third, and to all the others who were following the herds, saying: "This is what you are to say to Esau when you meet him!"

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: kposɔ vinɔ blaetɔ̃ kple wo viwo, nyinɔ blane kple nyitsu ewo, tedzinɔ blave kple tedzivi ewo.
translated: thirty female donkeys with their foals, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Eye wogblɔna ŋgɔgbetɔ bena: Ne nɔvinye ava do go wò, eye woabia wò bena: Ameka vi nènye, afika nèyina, eye nusiwo le ŋgɔwò la, ameka tɔe mahã la,
translated: And you shall say to the first one: When my brother comes to meet you and asks you: Whose son are you, where are you going, and whose are these things before you,

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Womagayɔ wò be Yakob akpɔ o, ke boŋ Israel, elabena ete kame kple Mawu kpakple ame, eye nete ŋui.
translated: And he said: You shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, because you have struggled with God and with men, and you have prevailed.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Eye Yakob na ŋkɔ teƒe la be Pniel, elabena egblɔ be: Mekpɔ Mawu ŋku me kple ŋku me: gake metsi agbe.
translated: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: for he said, I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Eye mekpɔ aƒenyiwo, tedziwo kple alẽwo, kluviwo kple kosiviwo, eye meɖo dɔlawo ɖo ɖe nye aƒetɔ gbɔ, be wòave nunye.
translated: I have acquired cattle, donkeys and sheep, male and female servants, and I have sent messengers to tell my lord, that I may find favor in your sight.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: nye medze wò nublanui, si nèkpɔ nam, kple wò anukware, si nẽɖi na wò dɔla la dometɔa ɖeke o; elabena nye ati sia metsɔ tso Yordan sia, ke azɔ la metrɔ zu asafokɔ eve.
translated: I am not worthy of all the kindness and faithfulness you have shown to your servant; for I crossed this Jordan with only my staff, but now I have become two camps.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Meɖe kuku, ɖem tso nɔvinye si me, tso Esau si me; elabena mele evɔ̃m be, ava dze dzinye, eye wòaɖe zi dadawo kple viwo dome.
translated: I pray thee, deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, and the mother with the children.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: kposɔ vinɔ blaetɔ̃ kple wo viwo, nyinɔ blane kple nyitsu ewo, tedzinɔ blave kple tedzivi ewo.
translated: thirty female donkeys with their foals, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: ekema naɖo ŋu bena. Wò dɔla Yakob ɖo nu siawo ɖe nye aƒetɔ Esau: eye eya ŋutɔ le mia megbe gbɔna.
translated: then you shall answer, 'They belong to your servant Jacob. They are a present sent to my lord Esau. And behold, he himself is behind us.'

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Yakob ɖeɖe tsi megbe. Tete ŋutsua ɖe va te kame kplii ʋuu, vaseɖe esime agu dze.
translated: Jacob refused to let go. Then the man came and wrestled with him fiercely, until dawn broke.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Eye esi Laban fo ŋdi kanya la, eɖuɖɔ nu na tɔgbuiayɔviwo kple vianyɔnuwo, eye wòyra wo. Emegbe la Laban dze mɔ gatrɔ yi enɔƒe.
translated: Early the next morning Laban kissed his grandchildren and his daughters and blessed them. Then Laban left and returned to his home.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Ale miagblɔ na nye aƒetɔ Esau enye si: Wò dɔla Yakob be, míagblɔ na wò bena: Menɔ Laban gbɔ, eye metsi anyi ʋuu vaseɖe fifi.
translated: And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall ye speak unto my lord Esau; Thy servant Jacob saith thus, I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed there until now.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Elabena egblɔ bena: Ne Esau ava dze akpa ɖeka dzi, eye woade zi wo me la, ekema akpa ɖeka nasi adzo. Yakob ƒe gbedodoɖa.
translated: For he said: If Esau comes to the one part and attacks them, then the other part will escape. Jacob's prayer.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: kposɔ vinɔ blaetɔ̃ kple wo viwo, nyinɔ blane kple nyitsu ewo, tedzinɔ blave kple tedzivi ewo.
translated: thirty female donkeys with their foals, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Eye wogblɔna ŋgɔgbetɔ bena: Ne nɔvinye ava do go wò, eye woabia wò bena: Ameka vi nènye, afika nèyina, eye nusiwo le ŋgɔwò la, ameka tɔe mahã la,
translated: And you shall say to the first one: When my brother comes to meet you and asks you saying: Whose servant are you, where are you going, and whose are these things before you,

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Eye esi wòna woɖe tsi wo kple nusiwo katã le esi, yi ɖe tɔa godo la,
translated: And when he had sent them away with all that they had, he went to the sea,

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Womagayɔ wò be Yakob akpɔ o, ke boŋ Israel, elabena ete kame kple Mawu kpakple ame, eye nete ŋui.
translated: And he said: You shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have struggled with God and with men, and you have prevailed.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Eye Yakob dze mɔ va Sukot, eye wòtu xɔ ɖe afima, ke ewɔ agbadɔ na eƒe lãhawo; eyata woyɔ afima bena Sukot.
translated: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house, and made booths for his cattle; therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.

ref: Shechem added, “Do this favor for me, and I'll give whatever you want.
text: Tete Sixem gblɔ na fofoa kple nɔviawo bena: Ne miave nunye la, ekema nusianu, si miabiam la, mana.
translated: Then Joseph said to his father and his brothers: If you love me, then whatever you ask me for, I will give.

ref: Ask anything, no matter how expensive. I'll do anything, just let me marry Dinah.”
text: Tabianu kple akpedanu, alesi nu miabiam la, mana, abe alesi miedi ene; mitsɔ ɖetugbivi la nam made ko.
translated: The dowry and the gift, whatever you ask of me, I will give, as much as you desire; only give me the young woman as a wife.

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Eye Yakob viŋutsuwo do ŋu na Sixem kple fofoa Xamor le aye me, elabena egblẽ wo nɔvinyɔnu Dina.
translated: Jacob's sons answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Negbe ne mianɔ abe miawo ene, eye mia dome ŋutsu sia ŋutsu natso aʋa la, eya ko hafi míalɔ̃.
translated: Unless you become like us, and every male among you is circumcised, only then will we agree.

ref: But if you don't agree to be circumcised, we'll take Dinah and leave this place.
text: Ke ne mieɖo to mi, eye mietso aʋa o la, ekema miaxɔ mía nɔvinyɔnu la adzoe.
translated: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our sister and go.

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.
text: Woha woƒe kesinɔnuwo katã, eye woɖe aboyo ɖeviwo kple nyɔnuwo siaa, eye woha woƒe xɔme nuwo hã.
translated: They plundered all their wealth, and took captive all their children and women, and also looted everything in their houses.

ref: Finally, they reached Bethel, also known as Luz.
text: Eye Yakob kple amesiwo katã le eŋu la, wova Lus, esia nye Betel, le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.
translated: So Jacob and all the people with him came to Luz, that is Bethel, in the land of Canaan.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: Eye Yakob na ŋkɔ teƒe, si Mawu ƒo nu kplii le la, bena Betel. Raxel ƒe ku.
translated: Jacob named the place where God spoke with him Bethel. Rachel's death.

ref: Esau, also known as Edom, had many descendants.
text: Esia enye Esau, si enye Edom, ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinya.
translated: This is the account of the descendants of Esau, who is Edom.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.
text: Eye Esau kplu srɔ̃awo, viaŋutsuwo kple vianyɔnuwo kple eƒe kluvi, siwo katã le eƒe me, kple eƒe lãhawo kple eƒe dɔwɔlãwo katã kpe ɖe eƒe kesinɔnu, siwo katã wòkpɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la ŋuti, eye woʋu le nɔvia Yakob gbɔ heyi ɖe Seir-nyigba dzi.
translated: Then Esau took his wives, his sons and daughters, and all the members of his household, as well as his livestock and all his other animals and all the goods he had acquired in Canaan, and moved to a land some distance from his brother Jacob to Seir.

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lotan ƒe viŋutsuwo nye: Xori kple Hemam; eye Lotan nɔvinyɔnu enye Timna.
translated: Lotan's sons were: Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Disan viŋutsuwo nye: Uz kple Aran.
translated: The third sons were: Uz and Aran.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Esiawo nye Xoritɔwo ƒe amegãwo: Amegã Lotan, amegã Sobal, amegã Zibeon, amegã Ana,
translated: These are the chiefs of the Horites: Chief Lotan, chief Shobal, chief Zibeon, chief Anah,

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: amegã Magdiel kple amegã Iram. Esiawo nye Edom-megãwo le woƒe nɔƒewo kple woawo ŋutɔwo ƒe anyigbawonu. Eya nye Esau, amesi nye Edomtɔwo ƒe fofo.
translated: chief Magdiel and chief Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom according to their settlements in the land they possessed. This is Esau, who is the father of the Edomites.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Eye Israel lɔ̃ Yosef wu viaŋutsu bubuawo katã, elabena edzii le eƒe amegãkuku me, eye wòtɔ awu ʋlaya nɛ.
translated: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he was the son of his old age, and he made him a coat of many colors.

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Gbe ɖeka esi nɔviawo yi wo fofo ƒe alẽwo kplɔ ge le Sixem la,
translated: One day when his brothers had gone to pasture their father's flocks at Shechem,

ref: pieces of silver they sold him to the Ishmaelites who took him to Egypt.
text: Mina miawui, eye miatsɔe aƒu gbe ɖe ɖoa ɖe me, eye míagblɔ bena, lã wodaa ɖe vuvui, ekema miakpɔ nusi eƒe drɔ̃e la ava zu.
translated: You shall keep it until the fourteenth day of this month, and the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it at twilight.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Esi Ruben see la, exɔ nya ɖe enu gblɔ bena: Gbeɖe, migana miawui o!
translated: When Reuben heard this, he intervened saying: No, let us not kill him!

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Tete Ruben gblɔ na wo bena: Migakɔ ʋua ɖeke ɖi o: ke boŋ mitsɔe de do kema, si le gbedzi la me, eye migade asi eŋu o! Elabena ele didim be, yeaxɔe le wo si, eye yeaɖoe ɖe fofoa.
translated: Then Reuben said to them: Do not shed any blood, but throw him into this pit that is in the wilderness, and do not lay a hand on him! For he was seeking to rescue him from them and return him to his father.

ref: When the Midianite merchants came by, Joseph's brothers took him out of the well, and for
